titleStmt.title: Letter from Joseph Brennan to John J. Farrell, 18 May 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Joseph Brennan

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: This is a letter from Joseph Brennan to John J. Farrell. In the letter Brennan states that he is writing on behalf of Chalmers in reference to a previous letter sent by Farrell and others regarding a theatre tax. Brennan states that an interview with Chalmers would serve no useful purpose in the matter.John J. Farrell (1876-1959) was the former lord mayor of Dublin and managing director of the Irish Kinematography Co. Sir Robert Chalmers (1858 - 1938) was sent to Ireland as replacement under-secretary for Sir Matthew Nathan, who had resigned along with Augustine Birrell, his chief secretary, following the outbreak of the Easter Rising in 1916.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers, CSO RP/1916/5611/8505/1

correspAction.placeName: 62 Upper Sackville Street (O'Connell Street), Dublin, Ireland

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
DUBLIN CASTLE, 18th May, 1916. Dear Sir, I am desired by Sir Robert Chalmers to acknow-
ledge the receipt of the letter of the 18th instant
signed by you and others suggesting an interview with
him on the question of the Theatre Tax. Sir Robert
understands that you desire to advocate the temporary
postponement of this duty in Dublin, in which case he
is satisfied that an interview will serve no useful
purpose as the date from which takes eg
prescribed by Act of Parliament. Yours faithfully,
J.B. Alderman J. J. Farrell, Managing Director, Irish Kinematograph Co., Ltd., 62, Upper Sackville Street, Dublin
